boundaries_beyond_codex.destination: Mparanza's hosted WhatsApp Business webhook service and durable tenant-scoped message store
boundaries_beyond_codex.purpose: Acquire new inbound messages for later client-scoped read-only retrieval after the professional links an official WhatsApp Business number
boundaries_beyond_codex.content: For WABA and phone-number identifiers manually verified by the single allowlisted private-draft operator, the service receives Meta's signed webhook, normalizes supported new inbound events with a numeric E.164 sender, and stores the pseudonymous owner key, business phone-number ID, sender phone and optional profile name, message timestamp and type, bounded text or caption, one-way digests of Meta message/reply identifiers, and an opaque source ID. It does not retain the raw webhook, document filenames, button payloads, location events, media identifiers or bytes, group messages marked with Meta's group_id, messages without a numeric sender phone, unsupported events, history-sync events, or outbound echoes.
boundaries_beyond_codex.retention: A daily cleanup deletes live connector message rows after their message timestamp passes 90 days, and account deletion removes that owner's live messages and bearer tokens immediately. The current repository does not establish infrastructure-backup retention; public Marketplace activation remains blocked until that deployment setting is documented consistently.
controls: Automatic receipt begins only after the professional chooses the WhatsApp Business route and the single trusted, allowlisted private-draft operator manually verifies and links the Meta WABA and phone-number identifiers outside Codex.

The service verifies X-Hub-Signature-256 over the exact raw body before JSON parsing, matches both signed WABA and phone-number IDs to one owner, stores a one-way digest for deduplication, and discards unknown accounts and unsupported events.

The first version ignores history, outbound echoes, group_id events, messages without a numeric sender phone, location events, and unsupported message types; it minimizes document/button events, stores no raw webhook or media identifier/bytes, and exposes no Meta send operation.

Every live-store access carries the pseudonymous owner key and current business phone-number ID; message search and fetch include both in the database predicate, account replacement clears prior live messages and tokens, and account deletion removes that owner's rows, pending codes, and access tokens.

boundaries_beyond_codex.destination: Mparanza's authenticated read-only MCP service and the user's selected ChatGPT/Codex account
boundaries_beyond_codex.purpose: Search and open retained inbound WhatsApp Business evidence for one explicitly selected client
boundaries_beyond_codex.content: The MCP receives one query containing a user-confirmed E.164 client phone, optional date bounds and compact topic terms. Search returns at most twenty metadata-only opaque results; fetch returns only a selected message's normalized text plus sender, timestamp, message type, direction, and opaque source metadata.
boundaries_beyond_codex.retention: The connector does not intentionally persist MCP search queries or tool responses. Source messages are not returned after 90 days and expired live rows follow the daily cleanup rule above. Ordinary hosting request-metadata retention is a deployment setting that the current repository does not establish and must be documented before production or public release.
controls: The user's explicit request to search the linked WhatsApp Business account is the route confirmation; Vera does not invoke the connector merely because a local archive or Gmail question was asked.
OAuth access tokens are opaque, stored only as hashes, time-limited, bound to the exact MCP resource and whatsapp:read scope, and resolved to the owner key on every tool call.

Search requires exactly one full client phone and rejects zero-client, multi-client, malformed, studio-wide, overlong, or excessive-term queries; fetch accepts only an opaque prior result and rechecks the owner key.
All exposed tools are annotated read-only and the service defines no send, reply, history-import, media-download, or other write tool.

security_controls.control: Verify Meta's SHA-256 HMAC over the exact bounded raw request body before parsing, routing, or storing any event.
security_controls.on_violation: Return 401 and do not parse or persist the request.

security_controls.control: Match both signed WABA and phone-number IDs, serialize account replacement against ingestion, and constrain reads by OAuth owner and current business phone-number ID.
security_controls.on_violation: Discard unknown or stale account events and return no cross-owner or prior-account source.

security_controls.control: Expose only status, exact one-client search, and opaque fetch; validate strict schemas, cap results, and treat message content as untrusted evidence.
security_controls.on_violation: Return a tool error without broadening client scope or invoking any write or embedded instruction.

security_controls.control: Require distinct stable tenant and OAuth secrets of at least 32 bytes, a strong browser-session secret, canonical auth links and trusted Hosts, S256 PKCE, exact client/redirect/resource/scope bindings, account-locked atomic code exchange, hashed bearer tokens, and a serialized bounded public registry that evicts only unused clients.
security_controls.on_violation: Fail closed with no code or token; rate or capacity excess returns a bounded 429 or 503.

security_controls.control: Hide messages after 90 days, purge expired live rows and OAuth records daily, and provide a CSRF-protected visible disconnect action that deletes live messages, pending codes, and tokens.
security_controls.on_violation: Expired rows are not returned; invalid deletion requests are rejected without changing the linked account.

security_controls.control: Require configured Postgres for hosted operation and permit SQLite only through an explicit local/test path.
security_controls.on_violation: Return storage unavailable instead of accepting WhatsApp content into an implicit local database.
